description:
Create a new MCP tool `diagnose_styling` that takes a component tagName and returns a comprehensive styling guide:

**What it returns:**
1. Available CSS custom properties with descriptions, default values, and example usage
2. Available CSS parts with descriptions and example `::part()` selectors
3. Theming approach detection (token-based, class-based, data-attribute)
4. Light/dark mode support status
5. Common styling anti-patterns specific to this component
6. A ready-to-use CSS snippet showing how to customize this component

**Implementation:**
- New handler: `src/handlers/styling-diagnostics.ts`
- New test: `tests/handlers/styling-diagnostics.test.ts` (TDD — write tests first)
- New tool registration in `src/tools/component.ts` (or new `src/tools/styling.ts`)
- Uses CEM data (cssProperties, cssParts) + source analysis for :host patterns
- Detects token naming convention (--sl-*, --md-*, --hx-*) to suggest correct property names

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Tool returns structured JSON with all 6 sections above
- For components with 0 CSS props/parts, returns guidance on alternative styling approaches
- Includes framework-specific styling examples (React inline styles vs CSS modules)
- Tests cover: component with parts+props, component with only props, component with neither
- Must pass lint, format, type-check
title: New MCP tool: diagnose_styling — Shadow DOM styling guide per component
